[]: Until You Cum to Me -- A Semenal Analysis of the Hospital Scene Post by Guy Nacks » Thu Jan 19, 2017 9:21 pm So, one thing that's always felt off to me about the hospital scene in EOE is that after Shinji's finished masturbating, we see his right hand covered in semen. The pattern of ejaculate on his hand actually makes little sense if he was masturbating the normal way (hand gripping shaft, pumping until ejaculation) because if he was, then the semen would shoot past his hand--which shouldn't have been covering the tip of his penis in the first place--and onto Asuka and/or her bedsheets. Yes, his penis would be smaller due to his age, but I don't think I ever masturbated at that age where my hand would be in a position get semen all over it upon ejaculaton. Which leads me to this theory: Shinji actually jacked off with his LEFT hand and used his RIGHT hand to deliberately PREVENT it from spraying on Asuka and/or her sheets. This might be due to his wanting to "save" Asuka from the perceived evil that is himself and reinforcing his protective nature of Asuka that we first saw in significant fashion during Magmadiver. Here he's once again "saving" Asuka from the perils of the world. He's the new (cum) Catcher in the Rye. I know this topic is ridiculous, but I'm actually semi-serious about it.

Shinji is fully aware that he can't go back on what he did and he has to hold that burden...all the way until the him and Asuka are alone on the beach. Shinji admitted that he was "terrible" to do that, but he can't do anything but live on.

[]: Re: Until You Cum to Me -- A Semenal Analysis of the Hospital Scene Post by ran1 » Fri Feb 03, 2017 1:35 am I was considering this at length about two hours ago and then thought it was stupid and my anecdotal reference was useless here but now I'm starting to think otherwise. In the West we have a sort of preconceived hand-signal that denotes masturbation -- a single hand moving up and down an imaginary cylinder. But when I went to go visit friends & a colleague in Japan two years ago, we went out to a bar and got roaring drunk on banana beer (no, really), and after a few dick jokes, my Japanese friend (from the Kansai region, specifically) made a reference to masturbation that involved grabbing a bottle with her right hand and then "covering" in a hovering motion the top of the bottle with her left hand, and then proceeding to move it up and down vigorously. Now I may be misremembering, but we had several of these bottles on the table that we were sitting at, empty, and I'm quite sure she had herself an empty one while she was doing this motion, so it would seem to me that this "hover-hand" might be something unique to the Japanese in the broader masturbation process. Y'know, societal politeness, et cetera. Otherwise why "cover" a bottle that's empty? So-- then I hopped on pornhub and paid particular attention to the male actors (no homo?) in three randomly chosen videos from the front page of the "Asian section" -- and whaddya know, one dude has both hands down while he's playing with his dong. No hover-hand specifically, but perhaps - perhaps, hand number two was down there out of reflex. Shinji could've done that hover-hand too, and wham -- spunkus handerruptus. Food for thought.

[]: Re: Until You Cum to Me -- A Semenal Analysis of the Hospital Scene Post by Reichu » Fri Feb 03, 2017 3:09 am Shinji is right-handed, yes. (Cf. cello playing, habitual fist-flexing, carry-over to Eva-01's weapon-wielding, etc.) In fact, I can't think of a single character in NGE whom they bothered to make left-handed -- not even an obvious choice for being sinistral like Kaworu. (Yes, I actually took the effort to make absolutely sure. I'm weird.)

[]: Re: Until You Cum to Me -- A Semenal Analysis of the Hospital Scene Post by NFWStateMachine » Tue Feb 21, 2017 3:11 pm I hope you enjoy my first post. As an experienced wanker, I can only speak for myself, but I would not be surprised if the same can be said by other males. I can spend at least an hour on the same jerk, prolonging the release of splooge. In doing so, a significant amount of love can preload in the shaft. Upon climax, all of the nectar releases at near maximum uncontrollable velocity and volume, being extremely satisfying until I realize I don't have enough toilet paper to cradle and contain the shame, for which the regret spills out on to my thighs and or the convulsions were so strong that some glue ended up on some perceptually important thing. This isn't a usual five to ten minute wank, like most males do. I'm sure it's similar to tantric sex. Having watched enough JAV, there is one series where I've seen this before where one male with a visibly flaccid penis (from prolonged pumping off-cam) ends up explosively venting all over a woman's face. This is very unlike most Western or European male actors that often have a piddly stream running out of their engorged third legs, despite having giant sacks, though they sometimes have empty bags as well. It's clear they don't fuck long enough and or they ejaculate so much during a shoot (off-cam, which I've seen in a documentary as a way of prolonging subsequent attempts at fucking without ejaculating due to being in a post-ejaculataion state where stimulation is 'harder' to do) that barely anything is left at the end for the money shot. Edit: I should make it clear that the above material is not something I condone, but I do think it's quite clear that people honestly enjoy things like that, if not on a daily basis, at least at some times in their lives. "Dirtying" themselves like Misato. I just feel that being honest is worthwhile more than not. It's not to say that Shinji does this exactly, but his form of it has an negative emotional blueprint rather than what is distinctly normally "positive" to the person or persons involved, as is usually the case in both series. If he doesn't get his way, ie. people he expects to communicate with him refuse to, he'll react violently and or shamefully. It's the one character trait you can set your watch to and will probably remain the same in the final film. And for Shinji, it doesn't matter whether the communication is positive or negative, he's obsessed with it and will almost forgive everything and anything as long they interact with him. It relates to an overall idea that humans (or any animal really) only have a pseudo-choice in interactions: Whether to vocalize and or literalize their thoughts or react violently, which has been and will continue to remain true for the foreseeable future. There are ample examples in both series, but the ones that are seemingly the most important are between Shinji and Asuka, namely her maliciously refusing to help him understand her, and him immediately choking her. Pretty obvious. Then, as he could only do, the very next time he interacts with her again we see the jerk off scene (actually, I might be inaccurate here and I can't remember the exact chronology of events as shown, but I'm pretty sure I'm right), then after that is them on the beach where he picks up where they left off last time they were having a two-sided argument, choking her 'lifeless' body, for which she touches his faces and shows actual growth, where Shinji again reverts into a sobbing mess having not really changed at all. The Hedgehog's Dilemma is a major theme (using animals as a metaphor for human relationships), but the binary way in which humans (or animals) communicate (rational conversation or other forms of 'polite' communication vs. physical violence) precedes it as a root 'issue' or 'problem' in the chain of causes and effects. Or in other words, the brain is the problem, or deeper still, the way in which organisms operate at some lower level is the problem. A cycle where it's just turtles all the way down (Mandelbrot set). Push and pull. Who learns or teaches first? And how violence is often the only option to stem or cull a destructive force. Of course, violence in itself is a sub-interaction under the main interaction of communication, but most people tend to think of them in some sort of binary relationship when what they really mean is, "positive communication" vs. "negative communication", even though those are often muddled in arbitrary definitions. It is my analysis that in a sense, true 'hatred' would be utter indifference and non-communication, which aligns with Shinji's often immaturely expressed and interpreted perceptions and resulting juvenile behaviour. In other words, despite people around him obviously communicating with him in 'good will', he finds ways to delve into his own selfish self-loathing that often triggers his masturbatory 'soul-searching' escapades and other sociopathic and violent behaviours, which should not be considered 'good' behaviour. So, when Asuka is despondent to Shinji's pathetic pleas for help, even when she's clearly unconscious, Shinji doesn't care, refuses to understand, and ends up violently pulling her over on her back, ripping her shirt open as well various monitoring electrodes that are aiding her recovery (which is important), exposing her jubs, and then jerkin' his gerkin' like the coward that he is (temporarily a coward, though that 'image' of him will always exist within him, ready to resurface at 'predetermined' points, so to speak). In summary: He violently flailed his second Adam enough that he ended up spraying the temporary warmth of his love on to his hand, until it became cold like his sociopathic and narcissistic heart in a crusty AT cloth (tissue) in a waste basket that is his mind. Seriously: He's shown to cum so much because it's a visual manifestation (by artistic design and in-universe function) of his pent up frustrations and how they all end up with him feeling and being 'marked' by shame among many other emotions and thoughts. I do like the idea that he tried to 'protect Asuka from his shame', which is probably the case and I never thought of the scene that way, but to diverge from the OP, I'd simply guess that he covered himself with the same hand he jerked off with (right hand). His soldier's convulsions were so strong that he didn't have to pump any more and as soon as he felt he was climaxing, he cupped his one-eyed Adam, but it wasn't enough to prevent tainting Asuka's vulnerable flesh (ie. other people, like everything else that he ever does in either series). To counter the idea of 'protection', has he really ever protected Asuka in any substantial way? How could he when he himself says that he doesn't understand her? Is he being honest to himself and others? Though I suppose in that case, he's always been lying to himself and that's the whole point of the original series for him to stop it and realize the truth, but he both does (ep. 26) and doesn't (EoE), at least that's always been my perception of it. Also, the cupping and massaging of a penis head can be seen quite often in certain AV, JAV, or hentai where the female is the 'master'. It's just short from sadistic/masochistic where its 'maliciously' done in combination with other 'denial' methods, like squeezing the shaft of the penis with the other hand or some device. That said, I don't think that specifically happened in this particular scene, but it would make sense if it did, at least Shinji cupping his dong while ejaculating to deny 'full pleasure'. Having finally watched 3.33 recently, Shinji's choker makes more obvious sense and it undoubtedly gave him a certain level of 'positive satisfaction' (because he loves any form of communication) especially when it was released and 'shared' by someone he 'loved'. He's always been submissive. It has to be intentional, because I don't see that as being a 'random choice'.
